site.title: The Friendly Manifesto
site.description: A voluntary set of principles for open-source Software Developers, Gatekeepers, and Users.
hero.developers: commit to building honest, secure, and respectful software that does not exploit its Users.
hero.gatekeepers: commit to distributing software fairly, transparently, and without subjective or ideological gatekeeping.
hero.users: commit to engaging with developers and communities respectfully, in good faith, and with realistic expectations.
badge.heading: Show your commitment
badge.description: Add this badge to your project or website to show that you adhere to the Friendly Manifesto.
footer.license.name: CC BY-SA 4.0
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: The Friendly Manifesto is a static website. We believe in practicing what we preach, so here's the short version: we don't track you.
privacy.noCollection.heading: What we collect
privacy.noCollection.body: Nothing. This website does not use cookies, analytics, tracking pixels, or any form of data collection. There are no forms, no accounts, and no personalization that requires storing your information.
privacy.hosting.heading: Hosting
privacy.hosting.body: This site is hosted on Amazon S3 and served through Amazon CloudFront. AWS may collect basic technical information such as your IP address in access logs. You can read more about how AWS handles data in
privacy.thirdParty.heading: Third-party resources
privacy.thirdParty.body: This site does not load fonts, scripts, or other resources from third-party servers. Everything is served from the same domain.
privacy.changes.heading: Changes to this policy
privacy.changes.body: If this ever changes, we'll update this page. But honestly, we don't plan on adding tracking. That would go against the whole point of this project.
